summary: Soundboard recording with occasional mix quirks captures a strong Reno show highlighted by a smoking Truckin' jam, Brown Eyed Women and Me & Bobby McGee.
review: The Charlie Miller‑sourced soundboard is generally clear—though the opening “Promised Land” mix is muddy and the tape runs fast, requiring minor pitch correction. Musically the performance shines with a landmark Truckin', tight Brown Eyed Women, an exceptional Me & Bobby McGee, and solid playing from Keith on keys, while The Other One suffers lyrical flubs and Sugar Magnolia feels shaky.

ai_rating.rationale: Three detailed reviews emphasize strong musical moments and a decent soundboard source; only minor technical flaws keep the rating just above four.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear overall, but opening track "Promised Land" is poorly mixed; tape runs fast requiring -1% to -2% pitch adjustments; occasional wind‑related ambience.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Presented as a three‑hour marathon (often interpreted as three sets) hampered by high winds and the massive Wall of Sound, yet the band kept momentum with tight jams and strong transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s vocals sound unusually warm; his lead guitar is playful throughout the China→Rider jam and he adds slide work on Truckin'.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh provides solid rumble cues, a powerful bass line during Truckin' (even teasing The Other One), though lyrical recall slips on The Other One.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocal delivery is rich and warm for most songs; however he flubs lyrics on The Other One.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s organ/piano work is highlighted as a standout, especially on U.S. Blues and throughout the set, contributing to the overall tightness.
